lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Whoah there, stranger, careful where you're walking. I almost clobbered you there; thought you were a larupia or something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Me? Oh, I'm just having some fun, capturing wild animals, living on the edge, stuff like that.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Hey, want me to teach you how to catch the critters around here? Ain't come across anything yet that I couldn't capture.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ok, where's a good place to start?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Your easiest bet around here is probably to go for some of the birds. Go for the crimson swifts; they're the bright red ones that hang around near the coast. They're as obliging as can be, or maybe they're just

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: plain dumb, but either way they just seem to throw themselves into traps half the time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What sort of traps should I use?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Just use a standard bird snare and you'll be fine. You can get them in any decent shop that sells Hunter gear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do they work, then?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Well, they're kind of sneaky, really. You've got a tall post with what appears to be a little perch sticking out the side. Now, the bird will see this as somewhere to sit, but the perch is actually rigged such that when the bird

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: lands on it, it'll drop away.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Further, as the perch drops, it releases a noose that tightens around the bird's feet and captures it. Neat huh?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: So, was there anything else you wanted to ask about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for your help.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Not a problem, any time.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What sort of things are there to catch around here?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: What're you interested in? You've got anything from birds for beginners up to larupias for the pros, not to mention weasels, butterflies, barb-tails and chinchompas.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How would I go about catching birds?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: There are a couple of varieties that you can try your hand at catching around here, but the technique for both is essentially the same. You need to set up a bird snare.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do they work, then?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Well, they're kind of sneaky, really. You've got a tall post with what appears to be a little perch sticking out the side. Now, the bird will see this as somewhere to sit, but the perch is actually rigged such that when the bird

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: lands on it, it'll drop away.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Further, as the perch drops, it releases a noose that tightens around the bird's feet and captures it. Neat, huh?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: So, was there anything else you wanted to ask about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I get hold of a weasel?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Not with large quantities of rice and treacle as many people would have you believe. Besides, the Feldip weasel isn't technically a weasel, but, as is typical with any sort of colloquial name, it's been called that often enough

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: now that it's stuck.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Anyway, the Feldip weasel's main survival technique is in hiding; other than that, it's pretty stupid. So, if you can actually find one, then you've probably got a fairly good chance of catching one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how would I actually go about finding one?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: The best place to start would probably be just south of here. You'll find lots of kebbit burrows there. Take a close look at some of them, follow the trails and you can't go far wrong.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, seeing as you seem to like larupias so much...

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Ah yes, yes, larupias are fun; just think of them as big kittens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh huh, big kittens that just want to rip your throat out!

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Indeed, and you can use this to your advantage. If a larupia is keen to chase you then let it; that way you can happily lead it straight into your trap without any fuss or waiting around.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Into what sort of trap should I lead it?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: A pit trap should do you. There are some appropriate pits to the east of here that you can use. Cover one with a few logs and branches, tease the kitty and then lead it to the pit. Simple.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Barb-tails sound interesting.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: They do sound interesting, don't they? Unfortunately, they're actually just another type of kebbit; those things get everywhere. Still, the tails of these particular kebbits can be useful for fishing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, how would I go about catching one them?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Just squash them with a boulder, quick and simple.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, you mean just pick up some boulders and start throwing them at kebbits? That doesn't sound simple.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: No silly, you let the creature squash itself. Just use some sticks to hold a boulder in a nice precarious position, place some bait underneath and let the creature knock itself out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What on earth is a chinchompa?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Awwww, chinchompas are great. They're highly volatile little balls of fluff. Cute as you like, but for some reason they do have this tendency to spontaneously combust.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Why?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Probably something to do with the fact they'll eat anything and everything, especially spicy food.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Anyways, if you want to capture a chinchompa, I suggest using a box trap. That way, if the little fluffball does explode, then at least most of the blast will be contained within the box.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Somehow butterflies don't strike me as your sort of thing.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Not really, but some people like them. The ones around here are at least vaguely interesting, though. The black ones are supposedly magical butterflies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Doesn't that make them harder to catch?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Not particularly; you can still catch them using an ordinary butterfly net. Oh, and you'll probably need some butterfly jars in which to keep them as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I improve my chances of making a capture?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: There are four key factors that will affect your chances: what bait you use, how close to the trap you are, your appearance and your smell.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How does bait help?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Unfortunately, creatures don't just jump into traps for the fun of it nearly as often as we as Hunters might like. However, it is possible to improve those odds. If you can find the right bait to use on your trap,

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: something your prey really craves, then hunger overrides fear and the creature is just that little bit more likely to bounce to its furry little doom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is it important how close I am to the trap?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: However well you might be blending in, if you're standing with your nose pressed right up against your own trap, the poor little critters ain't going to go anywhere near it. So, you need to make sure you put

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: a sensible distance - I'd say at least four strides - between yourself and your trap.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why would the creatures care about my appearance?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: You're a human. From the point of view of a lot of creatures, you're big and scary. So, it'd be best if the furry little monsters didn't notice you. You need to blend in like the creatures themselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And how do I do that?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Easy, dress up like one of them. Even easier, dress up using the furs of the creatures themselves; then you're bound to get the colour right. And, I suppose, if you don't fancy just slinging a dead fur over your

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: shoulders, you can probably get them made into something more appealing. I hear there are tailors in Varrock who are good at that sort of thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's wrong with my smell!?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: I'm sure there's nothing wrong with your smell...from a human point of view. But to your prey, any trap that you've set up will reek of human, and that's going to work against you if you don't do anything about it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what should I do?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: You need to mask that lovely human scent of yours with something else. Smoke works well. Take a lit torch and waft it around the trap a bit. You'll quickly overpower most of the other smells around the trap.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get clothes and equipment like yours?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Well, the equipment isn't too hard to get hold of. You've got specialist shops like Aleck's in Yanille that'll see you right. He's a bit pricey, mind, but it's pretty decent kit.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: The clothing is a tad trickier to get hold of, the materials required being rather difficult to seperate from their reluctant owners.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: But, if you can get the furs, there are shops like the tailor's in east Varrock that can help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'll give it a miss right now, thanks.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Suit yourself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's a larupia?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: What's a larupia? You don't know? You mean you really don't recognise the significance of these clothes I'm wearing?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, they're certainly decorative...what about them?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Well, these are the furs from a larupia. Caught the creature myself I did, and these make me blend in better, see?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: If you want to be successful as a hunter you've got to be as stealthy as a kyatt, as quiet as a mouse and blend in like a, well, like a larupia, I guess!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is that cool cape you're wearing?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: This? It's a Hunter's Skillcape. It shows that I am a true master of the art of Hunting and provides five daily teleports to either the Feldip Hills or Wilderness Hunter areas. If you ever manage to train your

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Hunter skill to its maximum level then I could sell you one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ahh, leave me alone you crazy killing person.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Heh, well I guess it's not everyone's thing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you give me some hunting traps?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: I do have a spare Hunter kit lying around, you can have it for free.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks! I will put this to good use.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you give me some hunting traps?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: I don't have any more Hunter kits lying around, sorry. Maybe you could try one of the many shops that sell hunting traps, you can find one in Yanille.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Is there anything else I can help you with?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I buy a Hunter's Skillcape from you?

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: I would be delighted to recognise you as a master hunter, the cape provides five daily teleports to either the Feldip Hills or Wilderness Hunter areas. I will need 99000 coins to cover the expense of making such a

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: costly item!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: 99000! That seems a bit expensive.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Actually it's quite reasonable; it's made from the fur of the nearly extinct Raharni wildcat. Well, if you change your mind you know where to find me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Certainly, I'm happy to pay that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But it seems that I don't have enough money with me.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Well, come back and see me when you do.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Unfortunately all Skillcapes are only available with a free hood, it's part of a skill promotion deal; buy one get one free, you know. So you'll need to free up some inventory space before I can sell you one.

lines.speaker: Hunting expert
lines.text: Now you look like a true hunter!
